summary: Large ty/toy.c matching pass. The PR body says it brought toy.c to 52/70 matched functions, typed and renamed the light-object loader as Toy_LoadLObjList, and verified with ninja progress and diff_changes.py. decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code at 66.98% (+0.23%, +8928 bytes), with 13 new toy.c matches and 18 further improvements. The slice contains no human review comments.

key_files.role: Main matching pass: 3520 added lines, 462 deleted lines, many placeholders replaced or improved, local structs expanded, Toy_LoadLObjList implemented/renamed, and matching tactics applied.

Shared prototypes and extern declarations updated; added sc/forward.h, LightList-aware Toy_LoadLObjList prototype, s32 signature fixes, HSD_GObj callback prototypes, PosArray/PosArrayFull, and toy.c externs.
Renamed symbol un_80306EEC at .text:0x80306EEC to Toy_LoadLObjList.
Updated light data type to LightList** and call site from un_80306EEC to Toy_LoadLObjList.
Updated scene light loading call site to use LightList** and Toy_LoadLObjList.
Updated trophy list light loading call site to use LightList** and Toy_LoadLObjList.

what_changed: Renamed and typed un_80306EEC as Toy_LoadLObjList(LightList** list, s32* hasAnim), updated GALE01 symbols.txt, toy.c internal uses, and external call sites in tydisplay.c, tyfigupon.c, and tylist.c.

Replaced placeholders or first-pass stubs with C for functions including un_80305058, un_80307470, un_803087F4, fn_80309404, fn_8030B530, fn_8030E110, un_8030FA50, un_8030FE48, un_80310B48, un_80311960, and un_80311AB0_OnEnter.

decomp.dev reported 13 new 100% matches: un_80311AB0_OnEnter, un_8030FA50, un_80311960, un_80311788, un_80312050, un_80310660, Trophy_SetUnlockState, un_803063D4, un_80305918, un_80308250, un_80307BA0, un_803084A0, and un_80308F04.

decomp.dev also reported major unmatched-item improvements such as fn_8030B530 to 84.94%, fn_80309404 to 91.49%, fn_8030E110 to 86.22%, un_80310B48 to 97.69%, un_803087F4 to 99.87%, un_8030FE48 to 96.74%, un_80305058 to 99.29%, and Toy_LoadLObjList to 96.73%.

Expanded toy/light/trophy local typing with structs such as ToyListEntry, TyDisplayData, TyLightData, TyLightFile, TyLightSymbolEntry, TyLightIndexEntry, TyCleanupObj, Toy26B8, Toy6E68, ToyEntryData, ToyParamEditor, and ToyTable.

Moved many externs out of toy.c into toy.h and refined function signatures, including un_803063D4(s32,...), un_803084A0(s32), fn_80309404(HSD_GObj*), fn_8030B530(HSD_GObj*), fn_8030E110(HSD_GObj*), un_80310B48(HSD_GObj*), and un_80311AB0_OnEnter(void*).
Reworked un_80312050 to use GXPosition3f32 and GXColor4u8 rather than manual volatile WG pipe writes, with stack padding adjusted for matching.
Added MWERKS-only local declarations and alternate non-MWERKS calls around un_8030813C, preserving MWCC matching while avoiding issue-check prototype errors.
The bot noted one regression in an unmatched item: main/melee/ty/toy .sdata changed from 68.38% to 60.00% (-5 bytes).
smart_moves: The semantic rename Toy_LoadLObjList was backed by type evidence from multiple callers: archives return LightList** scene lights, the function builds an HSD_LObj chain, and the optional hasAnim out-parameter controls animation setup.
The PR kept uncertain layout work match-friendly by naming known fields with offset comments and pads while retaining M2C_FIELD for still-opaque accesses.
A struct assignment, table = *(ToyTable*) un_803B8910, replaced manual word-copy code and was called out in the squash message as a way to match array-copy bdnz codegen.
Signature widening from s16 to s32 for un_803063D4 and un_803084A0 was applied consistently in toy.h and callers, reducing casts and matching caller codegen.
The MWERKS-vs-clang split for un_8030813C is a reusable compromise: local prototype quirks remain visible only to MWCC, while clang/issue-check gets prototype-valid calls.
The cleanup centralized toy.c extern declarations in toy.h, reducing duplicated local declarations after the code became typed enough to share.
The PR did not force semantic names where evidence was still weak: most functions remain address-based un_803.../fn_803..., with only the light-object loader receiving a real name.
decomp_lessons: Use call-site convergence before renaming: a function returning HSD_LObj* from LightList** scene-light data justified Toy_LoadLObjList across ty/toy, tydisplay, tyfigupon, and tylist.

For large state structs, expose proven fields at exact offsets and leave pads for the unknown ranges; this enabled readable accesses like TyDisplayData list links and Toy26B8 trophy fields without overclaiming the full layout.
When a header signature changes for matching, update all dependent call sites in the same PR to avoid stale casts and old untyped prototypes.
MWCC matching and clang semantic checking can require different declarations; guard MWCC-only local prototypes with __MWERKS__ and provide prototype-valid fallback calls.
Small codegen details mattered: stack PAD_STACK/UNUSED padding, local variable scope, int loop counters, and loop shape changes were all used to improve or finish matches.
M2C_FIELD is useful as a transitional tactic for opaque global blocks: it documents offset/type access while avoiding premature struct commitments.
Track progress bot regressions even in successful matching PRs; this one gained many text matches but had a small .sdata regression.
naming_conventions: Toy_LoadLObjList follows subsystem prefix + verb + object naming and replaced the address-style un_80306EEC in both symbols.txt and toy.h.
Address-based names were retained for functions without strong semantic evidence, such as un_80305058, fn_8030B530, fn_8030E110, and un_80310B48.

Local temporary structs use Ty/Toy prefixes plus semantic or offset-derived field names, for example TyLightData.gobj/archive, TyDisplayData.first_entry/last_entry/selected_entry/visible_count, and ToyListEntry.archive_name/symbol_name/trophy_id.
Header prototypes keep the project's address comments, such as /* 306EEC */ for Toy_LoadLObjList and /* 311AB0 */ for un_80311AB0_OnEnter.
Scene lifecycle suffixes like _OnEnter and _OnFrame were preserved for known callbacks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK and UNUSED locals to tune stack layout in functions such as un_80305058, un_80305B88, un_80308F04, and un_80312050.
Used M2C_FIELD for loads/stores into partially typed globals and state blobs where direct struct fields were not yet stable.
Used struct assignment for stack table copies to reproduce compiler loop/codegen patterns, specifically ToyTable in un_803109A0.
Used scoped variable declarations, local temporaries, and int loop counters to reduce callee-saved register pressure; these tactics were explicitly mentioned in the squash commit message.

Changed loops from manual pointer arithmetic or goto-heavy forms to forms that better matched MWCC output, such as the simpler forward/reverse copy in un_803067BC and break-based count handling in un_803053C4.
Used #pragma push/#pragma dont_inline off and #pragma peephole on around sensitive sections where codegen needed control.
Replaced some direct struct helper calls with inline/local versions and direct field writes where that matched expected HSD JObj/LObj codegen.
Used OSReport plus __assert forms in some assertions instead of HSD_ASSERTREPORT where the generated code required the lower-level assert pattern.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
PR body verification evidence: ninja progress and diff_changes.py.
Squash metadata also mentions verification with ninja progress, diff_changes.py, git diff --check, local melee-issues check, and pre-commit hook.
decomp.dev bot reported 13 new matches, 18 unmatched-item improvements, matched code +0.23% (+8928 bytes), and one .sdata regression.

The squash commit message explicitly called out reusable tactics: struct assignment for array copies (bdnz), removing cached globals, scoped variable declarations for stack layout, int loop counters, for-loop patterns, M2C_FIELD macros, moving extern declarations to toy.h, and hiding MWCC-only declarations from clang issue checking.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolve the main/melee/ty/toy .sdata regression reported in PR 2487.
Search for remaining old un_80306EEC references or untyped LightList** call sites after Toy_LoadLObjList.
Search subsequent ty/toy PRs for full matches of fn_8030B530, fn_80309404, fn_8030E110, un_80310B48, un_8030FE48, and un_80305058.
Search for reused __MWERKS__ local prototype workarounds around un_8030813C or similar clang issue-check conflicts.
Search for later promotions or renames of Toy26B8, Toy6E68, TyDisplayData, TyLightData, and ToyListEntry fields into shared ty/types headers.
